# Questions in Livestream 180

Live Stream #180: Quirky Square Dancing Publishing

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=651" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:51</a> | Are angels more like natural forces, animalistic instincts, or hyper-rational creatures? | He says they are a bit of all of these, though less like impersonal natural forces than untamable spirit beings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=1007" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:47</a> | Can angels who make a bad choice still be saved? | He says no, because angelic rebellion happens with full knowledge rather than under human-style deception. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=1355" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:35</a> | What did the Egyptian king do in the afterlife? | He says the king served as a scribe aboard the bark of Re rather than laboring like ordinary dead people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=1788" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:48</a> | What is an archangel? | He says an archangel is a high-ranking angelic being, though the exact kind of spirit creature is not clear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=2280" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:00</a> | How should "be fruitful and multiply" be applied today? | He treats it as a broad creation principle tied to filling and subduing the earth, not simply a narrow literal command in every case. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=3398" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:38</a> | How intense does heresy have to be before someone is no longer a Christian? | He says this is debated, but heresy involves denial rather than a mere aberration or mistake. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=4136" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:56</a> | Is trichinosis from the Americas? | He says its distribution is mainly in the Americas, with another patch in Southeast Asia. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=4710" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:30</a> | How do we know Tarshish is Spain and not Tarsus? | He leans toward Spain, mainly because the texts frame Tarshish as a place reached by sea. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=5970" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:30</a> | What makes black magic black? | He says black and white magic are pagan categories, and the Bible rejects both as legitimate spiritual practices. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:35</a> | If word order does not affect meaning in Greek, can it still add emphasis? | He says fronting can add emphasis in some cases, but not every word-order variation carries special meaning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6484" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:04</a> | Why do scholars often describe Revelation as John's literary commentary rather than an actual revelation? | He says that is a false either-or, since Revelation can both unveil and interpret older prophecy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6774" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:54</a> | Can he recommend a book, resource, or author on Babylon? | He mentions Alan Millard as someone he often used, while cautioning that Assyriology is not his field. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=6870" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:30</a> | Did the Greeks have papyrus? | Yes. He says papyrus was an Egyptian export that Greeks acquired through Mediterranean trade. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=7671" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:51</a> | What is the scriptural evidence for spirit creatures? | He points to angels, prophetic visions, Eden, and other biblical passages where spirit beings appear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=7853" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:53</a> | Are chaos monsters also spirit creatures? | Yes. He treats them as part of the spiritual side effects built into ancient cosmological thinking. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=8176" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:16</a> | If God knows what Satan will do and how he will stop him, why does Satan still go through with it? | He says Satan is a defeated enemy trying to delay the inevitable until the fullness of the Gentiles is complete. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=8335" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:55</a> | What do you make of skeptics who say God should not judge the world or Sodom, but should have stopped Satan or Hitler? | He says that standard is inconsistent because it criticizes divine judgment and demands it at the same time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=9765" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:45</a> | Why did God punish David's infant for David's crime? | He says God punished David rather than the infant, describing the death as God removing protection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=10982" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:02</a> | Is Margaret Barker's temple theory a problem for Old Testament theology? | He says no and treats temple theology as an important interpretive frame, especially for Genesis 1. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=12352" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:52</a> | How did Egyptians handle children with disabilities? | He says they sometimes provided aids such as crutches, litters, or limited sign communication depending on the disability and status. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=12788" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:08</a> | Did Michael Heiser write fiction? | Yes. He says Heiser used fiction as one way of engaging the culture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/poPNaIqdshw?t=14333" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:58:53</a> | Who were the Ophites? | He describes them as a Jewish and later Christian gnostic sect associated with serpent-focused teaching. |
